questions.scenario: A VM works because someone SSHed in and installed packages by hand. The replacement VM fails. What is the core design problem?

options.text: The server state was not captured in an image or repeatable startup automation.
The project needs more DNS zones.
The machine type name is too short.

questions.explanation: A healthy VM design can reproduce the server from an image plus automation.

questions.scenario: The security team requires a hardened OS baseline for import workers. Which VM planning artifact should name that baseline?

options.text: The image used to create the VM.

questions.explanation: The image is the operating system starting point for the VM.

questions.scenario: Which statements about VM images are healthy?

options.text: They define what exists before startup automation runs.
Custom images need a refresh and patch strategy.
They help avoid manual snowflake servers.

questions.explanation: Images are runtime starting points, not permission systems.
